2606.09122.title: Autonomous Incident Resolution at Hyperscale: An Agentic AI Architecture for Network Operations
2606.09122.paper_summary: Cloud network infrastructure at hyperscale presents unique operational challenges where traditional human-driven incident response cannot keep pace with the volume, velocity, and complexity of failures. This paper presents an agentic AI architecture for autonomous incident resolution in large-scale network operations. Our system employs a multi-agent orchestration framework where specialized AI agents collaborate to detect, diagnose, and remediate network incidents without human intervention. We describe the architectural principles, including hierarchical agent decomposition, skills-based tool invocation via standardized protocols, structured knowledge encoding from operational runbooks, progressive autonomy with safety boundaries, and closed-loop verification. The architecture has been deployed in production at a major cloud provider, demonstrating that agentic AI systems can achieve autonomous resolution rates exceeding 90% for common incident categories while maintaining safety guarantees through layered authorization and rollback mechanisms. We discuss design tradeoffs, failure modes, and lessons learned from operating autonomous AI agents at scale.
2606.09122.paper_summary_zh: 雲端網路基礎設施在超大規模下面臨獨特的操作挑戰，傳統的人為事件響應無法跟上故障的數量、速度和複雜性。本文提出了一種用於大規模網路操作的自主事件解決的代理 AI 架構。我們的系統採用多代理協調框架，專門的 AI 代理協作以檢測、診斷和修復網路事件，而無需人類介入。我們描述了架構原則，包括層級代理分解、通過標準化協議基於技能的工具調用、從操作手冊中結構化知識編碼、具有安全邊界的漸進式自主性，以及閉環驗證。該架構已在一家主要雲端提供商的生產環境中部署，證明了代理 AI 系統可以實現超過 90% 的常見事件類別的自主解決率，同時通過分層授權和回滾機制維持安全保證。我們討論了設計權衡、故障模式以及在大規模運行自主 AI 代理過程中所學到的經驗教訓。
2606.09122.author: Arun Malik et.al.
